intent: Break an accepted spec or plan into tracer-bullet tickets — vertical slices that each cut a narrow but complete path through every layer, demoable on their own, sized to one fresh context window, each declaring its blocking edges — then publish them so sessions can work the frontier.

state.source: object — the spec, plan, or conversation being decomposed
state.slices: array — proposed tickets: title, what it delivers end-to-end, blocked_by edges
state.wide_refactors: array — mechanical whole-codebase changes pulled out for expand–contract sequencing instead of slicing
state.verdict: string (approved|amended) | null — maintainer's call on granularity and edges

edges.when: codebase state and domain vocabulary gathered; prefactoring opportunities noted
every slice has a title, an end-to-end deliverable, and its blocking edges; wide refactors sequenced expand–contract
one ticket per slice published in dependency order, blockers first, edges wired

termination: tickets published with blocking edges wired and the frontier (tickets with no open blockers) non-empty, or the run aborts at the gate; publishing never modifies or closes the parent spec
